title: MCP DRAFT / ONE-OFF RUN PATH (SYSTEM-GAPS G1)
what_built: Two NEW MCP tools on mcp_face/server.py that fire an INLINE draft role spec WITHOUT registering/committing it, closing G1 (run_role/run_items take a registered role id string; a one-off bounded classify forced create_role -> a committed throwaway role + index race, OR dropping below the MCP). run_draft(draft_role: dict, ...) = the single-fire; run_draft_items(draft_role: dict, items: list, ...) = the MAP (the motivating grunt-offload P-11 primitive, 'classify these N candidates once'). Each takes a draft FIELD-SPEC dict (the SAME shape create_role/dry_run_role consume: {id, prompt_template, output_fields:[{name,type,description?}], op?, input_addresses?, requires?, ...}).
reuse: REUSE-DON'T-PARALLEL: the engine path is the EXISTING one. _resolve_role(dict) (already present in server.py) renders -> authoring.load_role_from_source -> tempdir RoleRegistry discover -> shutil.rmtree in a finally (NEVER writes roles/). From the resolved Role, the new tools call the SAME engine: run_draft -> _cog.run_role; run_draft_items -> _cog.run_items. Also factored the heavy wrapper TAILS into two shared helpers so run_role/run_draft (and run_items/run_draft_items) stay byte-identical: _fire_role_and_persist (turn_id -> input-addr resolve -> fire -> persist run://<turn>/<role> -> op.run emit_run_record w/ finish_reason+usage) and _run_items_and_shape (the MAP fan + ItemsResult shape). run_role/run_items were refactored to call these helpers (behaviour-preserving; not copy-paste).
floor: FLOOR HELD. The draft role is NEVER written to roles/ and NEVER committed (tempdir-only, rmtree'd). These tools emit run:// COMPUTATION only -- NO resolve/approve/dispatch, NO claude -p. Verified by assertion in the by-use run AND by the floor scan (cognition_governance_acceptance 42/42).
how_verified: BY USE (in-process import of mcp_face.server -- the new tool is not in THIS harness's deferred mcp__company__* set this session, so verified in-process, the path the FastMCP server itself calls; @mcp.tool() leaves the fn directly callable): (1) chat-4b resident (company gpu: 14.8/16 GB, chat-4b ~8 GB). (2) run_draft_items over 3 LITERAL string units with a tiny classify draft {output_fields:[{kind: str}]} -> 3 per-unit outputs returned at run://<turn>/oneoff_kind/<i>, each a dict with the kind field. With an explicit prompt + 128 tok the MAP returned 3 DISTINCT CORRECT classifications: {0:'animal',1:'number',2:'music'}. (A terse 1-line prompt + 64 tok first returned empty 'kind' strings -- investigated per look-at-output law: isolated to small-model prompt/token sensitivity, NOT a draft-path bug; run_draft single-fire with the same explicit prompt returns {'kind':'animal'}, finish_reason=stop.) (3) FLOOR asserted: git status roles/ BYTE-IDENTICAL before/after every draft fire; no new roles/*.py file appeared. (4) ADVERSARIAL: a bad id ('Bad-ID') and an unfireable spec (no prompt_template) both FAIL LOUD (AuthoringError / ValueError) and leak NO file. (5) Both tools registered on the live FastMCP server (mcp.list_tools()). (6) REGRESSION: mcp_engine_acceptance 35/35 (run_items map test passes; build-dispatch floor checks pass), mcp_use.py PROVEN (real stdio client loads/serves the server with the 2 new tools), cognition_governance_acceptance 42/42.
notes: FILE-DISJOINT confirmed: git diff touched ONLY mcp_face/server.py (+86/-3). All other dirty/untracked paths (MERGE-COORDINATION.md, design/*, roles/repo_digest.py, roles/verify_lens.py, RegistryProposals.tsx, seam-build.workflow.js) are OTHER concurrent sessions' work -- not touched by this lane. Did NOT edit roles/AGENTS.md (no new role added; nothing to reflect). Did NOT edit runtime/cognition.py|suite.py|roles.py (read-only reuse of _build_role/dry_run_role/run_role/run_items via _resolve_role + authoring.load_role_from_source). Did NOT commit (next loop beat commits). FOLLOW-UP (flagged, not built): serving these on the operator FE / bridge is a separate BRIDGE+FORM lane (the MCP face is the agent face); and G3 multi-var prompt substitution / G4 retrieve cascade ops remain open in SYSTEM-GAPS, untouched by G1. A PERMANENT draft-tool acceptance suite (tests/*.py) is a next-beat follow-up -- intentionally NOT added here because a test file is a second file that breaks this lane's file-disjoint ONLY-mcp_face/server.py constraint; verified by use this session + covered by the floor scan + the existing mcp suites.
